samples.claim: As documented in its Wikipedia entry, the game introduced modular hex-based boards, resource trading, and a victory-point system that rewarded strategic planning.
A Reddit discussion on favorite games frequently cites Catan as a foundational experience, with users noting how it "opened the door to modern gaming."
Its Wikipedia article) (though primarily about Alhambra, it contextualizes the tile-laying genre) demonstrates how the game simplified complex strategy into accessible, family-friendly gameplay.
Its Wikipedia entry) details how the game combined set collection with route-building on a map of North America.
A shopping product page for a related T-shirt demonstrates the game's cultural penetration beyond gaming circles.
Products like the Chess Board Table Game Board at $149.95 show that board games were still positioned as premium furniture items rather than mass-market entertainment.
The Optoma UHD30 Gaming Projector at $1,199.99 represents the competing entertainment technology that board games had to contend with.
A discussion about positive unpopular opinions reveals that while many gamers appreciated Catan's innovation, others found it "too random" compared to later designs.
The r/gaming thread about early game releases suggests that even in the late 1990s, the board game community was already developing sophisticated tastes.
The Wikipedia article on BoardGameGeek notes that these titles consistently rank among the site's most-played games, demonstrating their lasting appeal.
The game's Wikipedia entry (while general, it contextualizes Agricola within the broader board game evolution) shows how the game transformed farming into a tense, resource-constrained puzzle.
The game's Wikipedia article) (which discusses the broader Eurogame tradition) demonstrates how Stone Age made the mechanic accessible through clear iconography and straightforward scoring.

verdicts: Are exactly 4 eras defined chronologically (gateway / Eurogame / Kickstarter / app-hybrid)?
Is each era given >= 3 wiki + >= 3 shopping + >= 3 reddit citations?
Is the chronology genuinely chronological (E1 to E4 increasing year)?
Are >= 6 game families named across the timeline (Catan / Carcassonne / Pandemic / Gloomhaven / Wingspan / Scythe)?
Does the modern taxonomy split games by >= 6 mechanisms (worker / deck / area / hidden / legacy / coop)?
Does each taxonomy leaf cite a shopping URL + a wiki URL?
Does each myth cite >= 1 wiki URL + >= 1 reddit URL?
Are >= 30 shopping board game products cited with price + rating?
Are >= 30 reddit threads from at least 4 board game sub-forums cited?
Are all 10 mandatory wiki articles cited?
Is per-domain minimum met: >= 30 shopping, >= 20 reddit, >= 15 wiki?
Are all cited URLs markdown-linked and sandbox-resolvable?
Does the report avoid producing a TOP-10 ranking?
Does the report distinguish 'still in print' vs 'historical only' games?
Does the report cite Spiel des Jahres winners across multiple eras?
Does the report avoid chain-of-thought and start with E1?
Does each era include reddit-sourced community sentiment, not just product specs?
